what_it_does: This file registers a single MCP tool named `ingest` that feeds files into an embedded, queryable corpus by delegating to `suite.ingest_paths`. It accepts directories and explicit file paths, bounds each call with `max_files`, supports incremental skip and forced re-ingestion, and returns counts plus a continuation hint when more files remain.
observations: Thin wrapper around `Suite.ingest_paths`; the tool does not implement its own walk/embed logic.
Requires `roots` or `paths`; otherwise returns a usage error.
Default `max_files=50` and returns `remaining` so the caller can page through a large tree.
`force=False` skips already-ingested paths; the docstring notes this is path-keyed, so changed files need `force=True` until a sha-refresh pass is added.
Lineage fields (`project`, `session`, `round`) are passed through to every record and are not defaulted away.
The docstring states capture fails loudly if the embedder/GPU is unavailable, avoiding silent unembedded records.
Adds a `_hint` when `remaining > 0` telling the agent to call again with the same arguments.
standouts: Self-teaching response: `remaining` + `_hint` guide the agent to continue paging.
Incremental skip behavior is explicitly documented and caveated.
Loud failure requirement for the embedder/capture step.
conventions: Heavy docstring-first design with G21-style identifiers and `run://` pure-computation framing.
Declarative records: no resolve/approve/dispatch inside the tool.
Reuses the suite pipeline rather than parallelizing or reimplementing it.
concerns: Skip is path-keyed, not content-keyed, so edits to already-ingested files are invisible without `force=True`.
`roots` and `paths` default to mutable `list` values in the function signature, though they are immediately copied.
No validation that `max_files` is positive or within a sane range.
On missing inputs it returns an `error` string in the dict rather than raising, which callers must inspect.
notes: A bounded, lineage-aware MCP ingestion facade that pages through large codebases and tells the agent when to call again.
questions: What exact file types and junk patterns does `suite.ingest_paths` skip?
What spaces besides `repo` exist and how is `projection=space` validated?
How does the 'digest fan' and capture+embed pipeline handle embedder failures in practice?
What will the future sha-refresh pass look like and when will it land?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a thin, bounded, incremental ingestion tool that delegates to the suite pipeline.
apparent_themes: Lineage and provenance on every record.
Self-service agent tooling with built-in continuation hints.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give an autonomous agent a safe, pageable way to load a codebase into a queryable memory layer while keeping each call bounded and transparent about progress and failure modes.
novelty: The combination of a hard per-call file cap with an explicit 'call again with the same args' hint, plus the honest caveat about path-keyed incremental skip needing `force=True` for changed files.
contribution: A single, well-documented MCP ingestion endpoint that enforces bounded batches, preserves lineage, surfaces remaining work, and delegates embedding/capture to the suite pipeline.
summary_for_embedding: `mcp_face/tools/ingest.py` registers the `ingest` MCP tool, a bounded, lineage-aware wrapper around `suite.ingest_paths` that loads files into an embedded queryable corpus and pages through large trees via a `remaining` continuation hint.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to give agents a self-service, incremental, fail-loud way to ingest code/design files into a searchable exocortex corpus.

symbols.does: Registers the `ingest` MCP tool on the given server and returns the tool function.
symbols.description: A factory function that attaches the `ingest` tool to the MCP server, wiring its parameters through to the underlying `suite.ingest_paths` pipeline.

symbols.does: Validates inputs, calls `suite.ingest_paths` with lineage and bounds, and returns ingestion counts plus a continuation hint.
symbols.description: The MCP tool implementation that walks directories or ingests explicit files into the `repo` corpus, enforcing `max_files`, honoring incremental skip unless `force=True`, and adding a `_hint` when more files remain to ingest.
